stages.name: unit_tests
stages.evidence: test/ai_doctest_survivor_cli_test 'a mutation-aware report the tool produces resolves a real survivor via explain-survivor' produces the stable report exactly as `doctest --mutate` now does (mutation_experiment.mutateReportJson over the survived.md fixture), extracts a ds_ survivor_ref FROM the produced report, and asserts dc.runSurvivor resolves it (exit 0, classification doctest_survivor_missing_assertion). Before the fix the producer entry point mutateReportJson did not exist (the test did not compile), and the CLI persisted nothing for explain-survivor to read. Full suite green (502 tests).

stages.name: real_binary_end_to_end
stages.command: zig build && ./zig-out/bin/zentinel doctest --mutate --file test/fixtures/doctest/mutation/survived.md && ./zig-out/bin/zentinel doctest explain-survivor <ds_...> --ai-provider stub
stages.evidence: The built binary's `doctest --mutate` persisted a stable zentinel.doctest.report.v1 report to zig-out/zentinel/doctest/report.json containing a real `zig test`-produced survivor (ds_d11j6nvjc75dssk5a1ttvbpf85, a comparison_boundary documentation mutant). `doctest explain-survivor ds_d11j... --ai-provider stub` resolved and explained it (classification: doctest_survivor_missing_assertion, exit 0), while a bogus ds_ ref returned ZNTL_DOCTEST_SURVIVOR_NOT_FOUND -- proving real resolution over a report the tool actually produces. The previously-dead-end subcommand is now reachable. (Runtime output under gitignored zig-out/ was cleaned up.)

stages.name: mutation_checks
stages.reason: task 113 wires an existing producer (stableMutationRun) into the CLI and persists its report; the new coverage is proven by a module test (compile-fails without the producer entry point) plus a real-binary end-to-end run. Full self-mutation dogfood is gated to task 085.

residual_risk: The automated regression test exercises the producer->consumer chain at the module level (mutation_experiment.mutateReportJson -> ai.doctest_command.runSurvivor), which is what the CLI persists and reads; the CLI adapter wiring (runDoctestMutate persisting to the default path, runDoctestSurvivorAi reading it) is additionally verified by the recorded real-binary end-to-end run rather than a spawn-based automated test, because build.zig (needed to inject the binary path as in task 111) is not in this task's allowed_files.

The hardcoded fixtures-only path gate on `doctest --mutate` was removed; the explicit `--mutate` flag is the opt-in (a config-file opt-in key would require src/config.zig, out of this task's allowed_files, and is not required by the acceptance). `--mutate` runs the mutation-aware pass over any --file documentation, spawning real `zig test` per mutated snippet, so it is heavier than the non-mutating doctest commands and depends on `zig` being on PATH.

`doctest --mutate` persists to the default doctest report path (zig-out/zentinel/doctest/report.json) under the project root; the experimental mutation_experiment.run/toJson surface is unchanged and still covered by doctest_mutation_experiment_test, but the CLI no longer uses it (it uses the stable report).
